questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Along the $x$-axis, three charges $\frac{q}{2},-q$ and $\frac{q}{2}$ are placed at $x=0, x=a$ and $x=2 a$ respectively. The resultant electric potential at a point $P$ located at a distance $r$ from the charge $-q(a < r)$ is $\left(\varepsilon_0\right.$ is the permittivity of free space)
questions.solution:
As $r \gg a$, so $r>2 a$ So, potential at point $P$
$\begin{aligned} V & =\frac{1}{4 \pi \varepsilon_0}\left[\frac{q / 2}{r+a}-\frac{q}{r}+\frac{q / 2}{r-a}\right] \\ & =\frac{1}{4 \pi \varepsilon_0} \cdot \frac{q}{2}\left[\frac{1}{r+a}-\frac{2}{r}+\frac{1}{r-a}\right] \\ & =\frac{q}{8 \pi \varepsilon_0}\left[\frac{r(r-a)-2\left(r^2-a^2\right)+r(r+a)}{r\left(r^2-a^2\right)}\right] \\ & =\frac{q}{8 \pi \varepsilon_0} \cdot \frac{2 a^2}{r\left(r^2-a^2\right)} \\ & =\frac{q a^2}{4 \pi \varepsilon_0 r^3}(\text { as } r>a)\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A neutral sphere of radius <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>r</mi></math> and density <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>ρ</mi></math> is placed in a uniform electric field <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>E</mi></math> that exists on the earth's surface in the vertically upward direction. If the atomic number and the mass number of the material of the sphere are <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Z</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi></math> respectively, then the fraction of electrons that should be removed from the sphere for it to remain in equilibrium is [Assume that the sphere is able to hold the necessary charge without any leakage. Here <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>N</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></msub></math> -- Avogadro number]
questions.solution:
Let us assume that we remove n electrons from the sphere due to which the net charge on the sphere is q=ne

For the sphere to be in equilibrium

mg=qE=neE

43πr3ρg=neE

⇒n=4πr3ρg3eE
The number of moles of the metal in the sphere is

nmol=mA=4πr3ρ3A

So the number of metal atoms will be

nmetal atoms=4πr3ρ3A×NA

This means the total number of electrons in the sphere is

ntotal=4πr3ρNA3A×Z

Hence, the fraction is

nntotal=4πr3ρg3eE×3A4πr3ρNAZ

⇒nntotal=gAeENAZ
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A body of mass $1 \mathrm{~g}$ and carrying a charge $10^{-8} \mathrm{C}$ passes from two points $P$ and $Q . P$ and $Q$ are at electric potentials. $600 \mathrm{~V}$ and $0 \mathrm{~V}$, respectively. The velocity of the body at $Q$ is $20 \mathrm{cms}^{-1}$. It velocity in $\mathrm{ms}^{-1}$ at $P$ is
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Two small conducting balls of identical mass $20 \mathrm{~g}$ and identical charge $10^{-10} \mathrm{C}$ hang from non-conducting threads of length, $L=300 \mathrm{~cm}$. If the equilibrium separation of balls is $x$ and $x \ll L$ then the magnitude of $x$ is (Assume, $4 \pi \in_0=\frac{1}{9 \times 10^9} \mathrm{~F} / \mathrm{m}$ and $g=10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2$ )
questions.solution:
Given, mass of each spherical ball, $$ m=20 \mathrm{~g}=2 \times 10^{-2} \mathrm{~kg}, g=10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2 $$ charge on each spherical ball, $$ q_1=q_2=q=10^{-10} \mathrm{C} $$ and length of thread, $$ L=300 \mathrm{~cm}=3 \mathrm{~m} $$ According to the question,
Equilibrium at point $B$, $$ \begin{aligned} T \cos \theta & =m g \\ T \sin \theta & =F \\ T \sin \theta & =\frac{1}{4 \pi \varepsilon_0} \cdot \frac{q^2}{x^2} \end{aligned} $$ (From Coulomb's law) From Eqn. (i) and (ii), we get $$ \frac{T \sin \theta}{T \cos \theta}=\frac{\frac{1}{4 \pi \varepsilon_0} \cdot \frac{q^2}{x^2}}{m g} $$ $$ \left[\text { Given, } 4 \pi \varepsilon_0=\frac{1}{9 \times 10^9} \mathrm{C}^2 / \mathrm{N}-\mathrm{m}^2\right] $$ $\therefore m g \tan \theta=9 \times 10^9 \cdot \frac{q^2}{x^2}$ From $\triangle A B M$, $\tan \theta=\frac{\frac{x}{2}}{\sqrt{L^2-\left(\frac{x}{2}\right)^2}}=\frac{x}{\sqrt{4 L^2-x^2}}=\frac{x}{2 L} \quad(\because L>x)$ $\therefore$ From Eq. (iii), we get $$ m g \cdot \frac{x}{2 L}=\frac{9 \times 10^9 \times q^2}{x^2} $$ Putting the given values, we get $$ \begin{array}{rlrl} 2 \times 10^{-2} \times 10 \times \frac{x}{2 \times 3} & =\frac{9 \times 10^9 \times 10^{-20}}{x^2} \\ \Rightarrow \quad x^3 & =\frac{27}{10^{10}} \mathrm{~m} \\ \Rightarrow \quad x & \quad \frac{3}{10^3 \times 10^{1 / 3}} & =\frac{3}{10^{1 / 3}} \mathrm{~mm} \end{array} $$ Therefore, magnitude of equilibrium separation distance of balls is $\frac{3}{10^{1 / 3}} \mathrm{~mm}$.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: An infinitely long thin straight wire has uniform linear charge density of $\frac{1}{3} \mathrm{Cm}^{-1}$. Then, the magnitude of the electric intensity at a point $18 \mathrm{~cm}$ away is (given $\varepsilon_0=8.8 \times 10^{-12} \mathrm{C}^2 \mathrm{Nm}^{-2}$ )
questions.solution:
Charge density of long wire $\begin{aligned} \lambda & =\frac{1}{3} \mathrm{C}-\mathrm{m} \\ \text { and } \quad r & =18 \times 10^{-2} \mathrm{~m} \end{aligned}$
From Gauss theorem $\begin{gathered} \oint \overrightarrow{\mathbf{E}} d \overrightarrow{\mathbf{S}}=\frac{q}{\varepsilon_0} \\ E \oint d S=\frac{q}{\varepsilon_0} \end{gathered}$ or $\quad E \times 2 \pi r l=\frac{q}{\varepsilon_0}$ or $\quad E=\frac{q}{2 \pi \varepsilon_0 r l}=\frac{q / l}{2 \pi \varepsilon_0 r}$ $\begin{aligned} & =\frac{\lambda \times 2}{2 \pi \varepsilon_0 r \times 2}=\frac{\lambda \times 2}{4 \pi \varepsilon_0 r} \\ & =9 \times 10^9 \times \frac{1}{3} \times 2 \times \frac{1}{18 \times 10^{-2}} \\ & =\frac{1}{3} \times 10^{11}=0.33 \times 10^{11} \\ & =0.33 \times 10^{11} \mathrm{NC}^{-1}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The line AA' is on a charged infinite conducting plane which is perpendicular to the plane of the paper. The plane has a surface density of charge σ and B is a ball of mass m with a like charge of magnitude q. B is connected by a string from a point on the line AA'. The tangent of the angle θ formed between the line AA' and the string is (provided, the charge q does not affect the distribution of charge on conducting plate)
questions.solution:
The diagram is as follows,



The electric filed due to charged infinite conducting sheet is E=σε0

Now, force (electric force) on the charged ball is F=qE=σ·qε0

The resultant of electric force and mg balance the tension produced in the string.

So, tanθ=Femg=qσε0mg=qσε0mg
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Two uniformly charged nonconducting spheres, each of radius R, are fixed in a gravity free space as shown in the figure. If an electron is released at rest from the point A, then its speed just before striking the other sphere is [mass of electron = me]
questions.solution:
Using the conservation of mechanical energy, we get

KQeR-KQe9R=-KQeR+KQe9R+12mv2

-2KQe9R+2KQeR=12mv2

164πε0eQ9R=12mv2 ⇒ v=8Qe9πε0meR
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Three charges − q 1 , + q 2 and − q 3 are placed as shown in the figure. The x− component of the force on − q 1 is proportional to
questions.solution:
Force on ( − q 1 ) due to q 2 = q 1 q 2 4π ε 0 b 2

∴    F 1 = q 1 q 2 4 π ε 0 b 2    along q 1 q 2

Force on - q 1 due to - q 3 = (  q 1 ) (  q 3 ) 4 π ε 0 a 2

F 2 = q 1 q 3 4 π ε 0 a 2    as shown

F 2 > makes an angle of 90 0 - θ     with q 1 q 2



Resolved  part of F 2   along  q 1 q 2

= F 2 cos 90 0 −θ

= q 1 q 3 sin θ 4 π ε 0 a 2   along  q 1 q 2

∴    Total force on − q 1

               = [ q 1 q 2 4 π ε 0 b 2 + q 1 q 3 sin θ 4 π ε 0 a 2 ]   along x-axis

∴    x-component of force ∝ [ q 2 b 2 + q 3 a 2 sin θ ] .
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A conducting soap bubble having a radius <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>a</mi><mo>,</mo></math> thickness <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>t</mi><mfenced><mrow><mo><</mo><mo><</mo><mi>a</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> is charged to a potential <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>V</mi></math>. If now, the bubble collapses and forms a droplet, then the  potential of the droplet is
questions.solution:
Let q be the charge on the bubble, then

V = Kq a   (Here K = 1 4 π ε 0 )

∴ q = Va K

Let after collapsing, the radius of droplet becomes R, then equating the volume, we have

4 π a 2 t = 4 3 π R 3

∴ R = 3 a 2 t 1 /3

Now, potential of droplet will be

V ′ = Kq R

Substituting the values, we have

V ′ = K Va K 3 a 2 t 1 / 3

or  V ′ = V a 3 t 1 / 3
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Three identical charges, each $2 \mu \mathrm{C}$ lie at the vertices of a right angled triangle as shown in the figure. Forces on the charge at $B$ due to the charges at $A$ and $C$ respectively are $F_1$ and $F_2$. The angle between their resultant force and $F_2$ is
questions.solution:
From figure the net force $F_{\text {net }}$ due to $F_1$ and $F_2$ makes an angle $\theta$ with force $\mathrm{F}_2$
$\begin{aligned} & \tan \theta=\frac{\mathrm{F}_1}{\mathrm{~F}_2} \\ & \text { Also, } \mathrm{F}_1=k \cdot \frac{q_1 q_2}{(3)^2} \\ & \because \quad q_1=q_2=q_3=2 \mu \mathrm{C} \\ & \Rightarrow \quad \mathrm{F}_2=k \cdot \frac{q_1 q_3}{(4)^3} \\ & \therefore \quad \tan \theta=k \cdot \frac{q_1 q_2 /(3)^2}{q_1 q_3 /(4)^2}=\frac{(4)^2}{(3)^2}=\frac{16}{9} \\ & \therefore \quad \theta=\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{16}{9}\right) \end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
An infinite number of electric charges each equal to 5 nano-coulomb (magnitude) are placed along $X$-axis at $x=1 \mathrm{~cm}, x=2 \mathrm{~cm}$, $x=4 \mathrm{~cm}, x=8 \mathrm{~cm} . . .$. and so on. In this set up if the consecutive charges have opposite sign, then the electric field in newton/coulomb at $x=0$ is :
$\left(\frac{1}{4 \pi \varepsilon_0}=9 \times 10^9 \mathrm{~N}-\mathrm{m}^2 / \mathrm{C}^2\right)$
questions.solution:
Electric field intensity due to a point charge. $E=\frac{1}{4 \pi \varepsilon_0} \cdot \frac{Q}{r^2}$
The consecutive charges are of opposite signs. $\therefore$ Net electric field at $x=0$, is $E=\frac{1}{4 \pi \varepsilon_0}\left[\frac{Q}{r_1^2}-\frac{Q}{r_2^2}+\frac{Q}{r_3^2}-\frac{Q}{r_4^2} \ldots . . \infty\right]$ $=\frac{Q}{4 \pi \varepsilon_0}\left[\frac{1}{r_1^2}-\frac{1}{r_2^2}+\frac{1}{r_3^2}-\frac{1}{r_4^2}+\ldots . \infty\right\rceil$ $=9 \times 10^9 \times 5 \times 10^{-9}$ $\left[\frac{1}{\left(1 \times 10^{-2}\right)^2}-\frac{1}{\left(2 \times 10^{-2}\right)^2}+\frac{1}{\left(4 \times 10^{-2}\right)^2}+\ldots \infty\right\rfloor$ $=\frac{45}{10^{-4}}\left[\frac{1}{1^2}-\frac{1}{2^2}+\frac{1}{4^2}-\frac{1}{8^2}+\ldots . \infty\right]$ $=45 \times 10^4\left[\frac{1}{1^2}-\frac{1}{2^2}+\frac{1}{4^2}-\frac{1}{8^2} \ldots \infty\right]$ $\left\lceil\frac{1}{1^2}-\frac{1}{2^2}+\frac{1}{4^2}-\frac{1}{8^2} \ldots \infty\right\rfloor=\frac{\frac{1}{1^2}}{1-\left(\frac{-1}{2}\right)^2}$ $\left[\right.$ Sum of infinite GP, $\left.\mathrm{S}_{\infty}=\frac{a}{1-r}\right]$ $=\frac{1}{1+\frac{1}{4}}=\frac{4}{5}$ $\therefore \quad E=45 \times 10^4 \times \frac{4}{5}=36 \times 10^4 \mathrm{~N} / \mathrm{C}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: An electric dipole is placed at the origin and is directed along the x-axis. At a point P, far away from the dipole, the electric field is parallel to the y-axis. OP makes an angle θ with the x-axis, then
questions.solution:
Direction of the electric field from the axis of the dipole is given by = θ + tan - 1 tan θ 2

Here given that E is along the y-axis.

θ + tan - 1 tan θ 2 = π 2





tan θ 2 = cot θ

tan θ = 2 cot θ  ⇒  tan 2 θ = 2

∴    tan θ = 2
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Three identical metal plates with large surface areas are kept parallel to each other as shown in the figure. The leftmost plate is given a charge Q, the rightmost a charge -2Q and the middle one remains neutral. Find the charge appearing on the outer surface of the rightmost plate.
questions.solution:
Let the charge distribution on the plates is as shown in the figure.

Electric field due to a charged surface having area A and  carrying charge q is 

E=q2Aε0

Let's consider right direction to be positive and left direction to be negative.

We know that electric field inside a conductor or metal is zero. Hence, equating electric field at point P inside the right most plate as zero.

Q+X2AεO+X2AεO-X2AεO+X2AεO-X2AεO+2Q+X2AεO=0

Q+X2AεO+2Q+X2AεO=0

3Q+2X=0

X=-3Q2

∴ Charge on outer surface of leftmost plate is

=-X-2Q

=3Q2-2Q

=-Q2
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
The uniform electric field intensity between the two plates of a parallel plate capacitor is $1 \times 103 \mathrm{Vm}^{-1}$ acting vertically upwards as shown in the figure.
The plates are sufficiently long and have separation $2 \mathrm{~cm}$. A particle of negative charge $1 \mu \mathrm{C}$ and mass $2 \mathrm{~g}$ is projected at an angle $45^{\circ}$ with the electric field from the lower plate with a velocity ' $u$ '. The maximum velocity acquired by the particle, if it is not hit the upper plate is
questions.solution: We have, $$ \begin{aligned} & h_{\max }=\frac{u^2 \sin ^2 \theta}{2\left(\frac{q E}{m}\right)}\left[\text { Here } g \rightarrow \frac{q E}{m}\right] \\ & u^2=\frac{2 h m q E}{m \sin ^2 45^{\circ}} \quad u^2=\frac{2 \times 2 \times 10^{-2} \times 10^{-6} \times 10^3}{2 \times 10^{-3} \times 1 / 2} \\ & \Rightarrow u^2=0.04 \Rightarrow u=0.2 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s} \end{aligned} $$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Two charged identical metal spheres $A$ and $B$ repel each other with a force of $3 \times 10^{-5} \mathrm{~N}$. Another identical wncharged sphere $C$ is touched with sphere $A$ and then it is placed mid-way between $A$ and $B$. Then, the magnitude of net force on $C$ is
questions.solution:
Spheres $A$ and $B$ have the same charge of same nature. When uncharged sphere $C$ is touched with sphere $A$, then charge is transferred to $C$ and this charge is equally divided between two spheres.
Let initially the charge on spheres $A$ and $B$ is $q$. Initially, the repelling force between charged spheres $A$ and $B=3 \times 10^{-5} \mathrm{~N}$ When, sphere $C$, is placed between charged spheres $A$ and $B$, then the force between $A$ and $C$, $$ F_{A C}=\frac{k \frac{q}{2} \times q / 2}{(r / 2)^2} $$ Similarly, between $B$ and $C$ $$ F_{B C}=\frac{k q \times q / 2}{(r / 2)^2} $$ The magnitude of net force on $C, F^{\prime}=F_{B C}-F_{A C}$ Also $$ F=\frac{k q \times q}{r^2}=3 \times 10^{-5} \mathrm{~N} $$ So, $\frac{F}{F_{B C}-F_{A C}}=\frac{\left(k q^2 / r^2\right)}{k\left[\frac{q \times q / 2}{(r / 2)^2}-\frac{q / 2 \times q / 2}{(r / 2)^2}\right]}$ Here, $\quad F=3 \times 10^{-5} \mathrm{~N}, F_{B C}-F_{A C}=F^{\prime}$ $$ \begin{aligned} & \frac{3 \times 10^{-5}}{F^{\prime}} & =\frac{1}{2-1}=\frac{1}{1}=1 \\ \therefore \quad & F^{\prime} & =3 \times 10^{-5} \mathrm{~N} \end{aligned} $$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: In many situations, to find the electric field using the gauss law, we can use the simplified equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced close="|" open="|"><mover><mi>E</mi><mo>→</mo></mover></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mfrac><msub><mi>q</mi><mi>enclosed</mi></msub><mrow><msub><mi>ε</mi><mn>0</mn></msub><mfenced close="|" open="|"><mover><mi>A</mi><mo>→</mo></mover></mfenced></mrow></mfrac></math>, where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>ε</mi><mn>0</mn></msub></math> is the permittivity of free space, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi></math> is the area of Gaussian surface and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>q</mi><mi>enclosed</mi></msub></math> is the net charge enclosed by the Gaussian surface. Among the following situations, this equation can be used
options: only when the Gaussian surface is an equipotential surface
only when <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced close="|" open="|"><mover><mi>E</mi><mo>→</mo></mover></mfenced></math> is constant on the surface

only when the Gaussian surface is an equipotential surface and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced close="|" open="|"><mover><mi>E</mi><mo>→</mo></mover></mfenced></math> is constant of the surface
for any choice of Gaussian surface
questions.solution: Magnitude of electric field is constant $\&$ the surface is equipotential
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: In air, a charged soap bubble of radius r is in equilibrium, outside and inside pressures being equal. The charge on the drop is $\left(\varepsilon_{0}=\right.$ permittivity of free space, $T=$ surface tension of soap solution),
questions.solution:
For a charged soap bubble 

Pexternal + Pelectric=4Tr

We also know that electrostatic pressure on the patch(small part of the charged surface) 

Electrostatic Pressure=σ22ε0

In equilibrium condition, equating above equations

σ22ε0=4Tr

σ2=8ε0Tr 

⇒σ=8ε0Tr    σ=QA, where A is surface area of spherical soap bubble.

⇒Q4πr2=8ε0Tr

⇒Q=4πr28ε0Tr
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The electrostatic potential inside a charged sphere is given as $V=A r^2+B$, where $r$ is the distance from the centre of the sphere, $A$ and $B$ are constants. Then, the charge density in the sphere is
questions.solution:
Let the volume charge density be $\rho$. The electric field inside the charged sphere,
$$ E=\frac{K q r}{R^3} $$ where, $R=$ Radius of charged sphere $r=$ Distance of the point $(P)$ inside the sphere As volume charge density is $\rho$, then in terms of charge density, $$ \begin{aligned} E & =\frac{\rho r}{3 \varepsilon_0} \\ \quad \quad \quad \rho & =\frac{3 E \varepsilon_0}{r} \end{aligned} $$ Now, electric field, $E=-\frac{d V}{d r}$ Here, $V=A r^2+B$ $$ \begin{array}{ll} \therefore & E=-\frac{d V}{d r}=-2 A r+0=-2 A r \\ \therefore & \rho=\frac{3 E \varepsilon_0}{r} \end{array} $$ $$ =\frac{3\left(-2 A r \cdot \varepsilon_0\right)}{r}=-6 A \varepsilon_0 $$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A charge (-q, m) is projected with an initial velocity v0 in the direction of a unidirectional field E0x as shown in the figure. Find the distance covered by the charge before it comes to rest.
questions.solution:
Since the charge is negative and is projected along the electric field, it will experience a retardation qE0m.

Let the charge travel a distance s before coming to rest (v=0). Using the third equation of motion,

v2=u2+2as

u2=-2as

⇒ v02=2qE0sm

⇒ s=v0m2qE0.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Two charge metallic spheres are joined by a very thin metal wire. If the radius of the larger sphere is four times that of the smaller sphere, the electric field near the larger sphere is
options: twice that near the smaller sphere
quarter of that near the smaller sphere
same as that near the smaller sphere
half of that near smaller sphere
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A simple pendulum with a bob of mass $40 \mathrm{~g}$ and charge $+2 \mu \mathrm{C}$ makes 20 oscillation in $44 \mathrm{~s}$. A vertical electric field magnitude $4.2 \times 10^4 \mathrm{NC}^{-1}$ pointing downward is applied. The time taken by the pendulum to make 15 oscillation in the electric field is (acceleration due to gravity $=10 \mathrm{~ms}^{-2}$ )
questions.solution:
Mass, $m=40 \mathrm{~g}=0.04 \mathrm{~kg}, q=2 \times 10^{-6} \mathrm{C}$
Now, $\quad m a=q E$
$$
\begin{aligned}
\Rightarrow \quad a & =\frac{q}{m} E \\
& =\frac{2 \times 10^{-6}}{0.04} \times 4.2 \times 10^4 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2 \\
& =2.1 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2(\text { downward })
\end{aligned}
$$
So, effective acceleration on bob,
$$
a_e=a+g=12.1 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2
$$
In the absence of electric field,
$$
T=2 \pi \sqrt{\frac{l}{g}}=2 \pi \sqrt{\frac{l}{10}}
$$
In the presence of electric field,
$$
\begin{aligned}
T^{\prime} & =2 \pi \sqrt{\frac{l}{a_e}}=2 \pi \sqrt{\frac{l}{12.1}} \\
\frac{T}{T^{\prime}} & =\sqrt{\frac{12.1}{10}}=\frac{11}{10} \Rightarrow T^{\prime}=\frac{10}{11} T
\end{aligned}
$$
Given, $\quad T=\frac{44}{20} \Rightarrow T^{\prime}=\frac{10}{11} \times \frac{44}{20}=2 \mathrm{~s}$
So, time taken in 15 oscillations
$$
=2 \times 15=30 \mathrm{~s}
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: An inclined plane making an angle \(30^{\circ}\) with the horizontal is placed in a uniform horizontal electric field of \(100 \mathrm{Vm}^{-1}\) as shown in the figure. A small block of mass \(\mathrm{I} \mathrm{kg}\) and charge, 0.01C is allowed to slide down from rest from a height, \(h=1 \mathrm{~m}\). If the coefficient of friction is 0.2 , then the acceleration of the block is nearly, (Acceleration due to gravity, \(g=10 \mathrm{~ms}^{-2}\) )
questions.solution:
According to the question, an inclined plane is making an angle of \(30^{\circ}\) with the horizontal, placed in a uniform electric field of \(100 \mathrm{~nm}^{-1}\). It can be such in the figure that a block of mass \(m\) is sliding down from rest at height \(h\).
From the above diagram, the total force \(F\) acting along inclined plane. From fig, \(m g \sin 30^{\circ}-\mu m g \cos 30^{\circ}-q E \cos 30^{\circ}=m a=F\) Given, \(\mu=0.2, m=1 \mathrm{~kg},, q=0.01 \mathrm{C}\) and \(h=1 \mathrm{~m}\) Putting these values, we get \(\begin{aligned} & 10 \times \frac{1}{2}-0.2 \times 10 \times \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}-0.01 \times 100 \times \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}=a \\ & a=5-\sqrt{3}-0.5 \sqrt{3} \approx 2.3 \mathrm{~ms}^{-2} \end{aligned}\) Hence, the acceleration of the block is nearly, \(2.3 \mathrm{~ms}^{-2}\).
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A hollow cylinder has a charge q coulomb within it. If ϕ is the electric flux in units of Vm associated with the curved surface B , the flux linked with the plane surface A in units of Vm will be
questions.solution:
Let   ϕ A ,  ϕ B   and   ϕ C  are the electric flux linked with A, B and C .

According to gauss theorem, ϕ total = ∑q ε 0

ϕ A + ϕ B + ϕ C = q ε 0

Since  ϕ A = ϕ C ,

∴     2 ϕ A + ϕ B = q ε 0

or      2 ϕ A = q ε 0 - ϕ B   

or    2 ϕ A = q ε 0 - ϕ

(Given ϕ B = ϕ )

∴     ϕ A = 1 2 q ε 0 - ϕ
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.solution: As we are are moving away from P toward sheet S spacing between electric lines of force is increasing.  ∴ER<EQ In direction of electric field potential decreases. ∴VR < VQ

The electric field <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>E</mi></math> varies with distance <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>r</mi></math> (from the centre of the shell) as

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The volume charge density in a spherical ball of radius $R$ varies with distance $r$ from the centre as $\rho(r)=\rho_0\left[1-\left(\frac{r}{R}\right)^3\right]$, where, $\rho_0$ is a constant. The radius at which the field would be maximum is
questions.solution:
Given, $\quad \rho(r)=\rho_0\left[1-\left(\frac{r}{R}\right)^3\right]$
So, effective charge at $r$ is $q(r)=\int \rho(r) \cdot V$
$$
=\rho_0\left[1-\left(\frac{r}{R}\right)^3\right] \frac{4}{3} \pi r^3
$$
For maximum value of field,
$$
\begin{array}{rlrl} 
& \frac{d q(r)}{d r} & =0 \\
\Rightarrow & \frac{4}{3} \pi \rho_0\left[3 r^2\left(1-\frac{r^3}{R^3}\right)+r^3\left(-\frac{3 r^2}{R^3}\right)\right] & =0 \\
\Rightarrow & & 3 r^2-\frac{3 r^5}{R^3}-\frac{3 r^5}{R^3} & =0 \\
\Rightarrow & r^3 & =\frac{R^3}{2} \Rightarrow r & =\frac{R}{2^{1 / 3}}
\end{array}
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

The electric flux through a spherical surface concentric with the spherical shell and of radius greater than that of the shell in V-m is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A spring block system undergoes vertical oscillations above a large horizontal metal sheet with a uniform positive charge. The time period of the oscillation is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>T</mi></math>. If the block is given a charge <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Q</mi></math>, its time period of oscillation will be
options: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>></mo><mi>T</mi></math> if <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Q</mi></math> is <math><mo>+</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">v</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">e</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo><</mo><mi>T</mi></math> if <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Q</mi></math> is <math><mo>-</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">v</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">e</mi></math>.
questions.solution:
Here, the electric force on block due to charged metal sheet is constant,

i.e., FElectric=QE=Qσ2ε0=constant
where,
σ→Charge density of metal sheet
Now, 
effective force constant (K) does not depend on the constant forces. So, time period of given system will remain same as T=2πmK sec.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A cube of side $L$ has point charges $+q$ located at its seven vertices and $-q$ at remaining one vertex. The electric field at its centre is found to be $|\mathbf{E}|=\alpha\left(\frac{q}{4 \pi \varepsilon_0 L^2}\right)$. The magnitude of constant $\alpha$ is
questions.solution:
The given situation is shown in the following figure
Clearly, $5 x$-components of $\mathbf{E}$ will be left to right, while $3 x$-components of $\mathbf{E}$ will be from right to left. From the figure given below
$r^2=\frac{L^2}{4}+\frac{L^2}{2}=\frac{3 L^2}{4}$ So, $E$ due to one point charge $|E|=\frac{K q}{r^2}=\frac{4}{3} \frac{K q}{L^2}$ Electric field at the centre of cube due to contribution of all charges is given as $|E|_{\text {Total }}=\frac{4}{3} \frac{K q}{r^2}(5-3)=\frac{8}{3} \frac{K q}{r^2}=\frac{8}{3}\left(\frac{q}{4 \pi \varepsilon_0 L^2}\right)$ Given,$|E|=\alpha\left[\frac{q}{4 \pi \varepsilon_0 L^2}\right] \quad\left[\because r^2=\frac{3 L^2}{4}\right]$ Clearly, $\alpha=\frac{8}{3}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A proton, a deutron and an $\alpha$-particle having the same momentum, enters a region of uniform electric field between the parallel plates of a capacitor. The electric field is perpendicular to the initial path of the particles. Then the ratio of deflections suffered by them is
questions.solution:
The deflection suffered by charged particle in an electric field is
$y=\frac{q E L D}{m \mu^2}=\frac{q E L D}{p^2 / m} \quad(p=m u)$
$\Rightarrow \quad y \propto \frac{q m}{p^2}$
$\Rightarrow y_p: y_d: y_\alpha=\frac{q_p m_p}{p_p^2}: \frac{q_d m_d}{p_d^2}: \frac{q_\alpha m_\alpha}{p_a^2}$
Since, $\quad p_\alpha=p_d=p_p \quad$ (given)
$m_p: m_d: m_\alpha=1: 2: 4$
and $\quad q_1: q_d: q_\alpha=1: 1: 2$
$\Rightarrow \quad y_b: y_d: y_\alpha=1 \times 1: 1 \times 2: 2 \times 4$
$=1: 2: 8$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Four electric charges $+q,+q,-q$ and $-q$ are placed in order at the corners of a square of side $2 r$. The electric potential at a point midway between the two negative charges is
questions.solution: Consider the following figure: $D P=P C=r$ and $A P=B P=\sqrt{A D^2+D P^2}=\sqrt{r^2+(2 r)^2}=\sqrt{5} r$ Potential at $P$ is $V_P=\frac{1}{4 \pi \varepsilon_0}\left[\frac{q}{A P}+\frac{-q}{C P}+\frac{-q}{D P}+\frac{q}{B P}\right]$ $\Rightarrow V_A=\frac{1}{4 \pi \varepsilon_0} V_A\left[\frac{-q}{r}+\frac{+q}{r \sqrt{5}}+\frac{+q}{r \sqrt{5 L}}+\frac{-q}{r}\right]=\frac{1}{4 \pi \varepsilon_0} \frac{2 q}{r}\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{5}}-1\right)$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: An electric dipole consists of two particle each of mass $1 \mathrm{~kg}$ separated by $\mathrm{l} \mathrm{m}$ carrying charges $1 \mu \mathrm{C}$ and $-1 \mu \mathrm{C}$ respectively. It is in equilibrium in a uniform electric field of $2 \times 10^4 \mathrm{Vm}^{-1}$. If it is deflected by a small angle $2^{\circ}$, minimum time taken by it to come back again to the mean position is (in seconds)
questions.solution:
Torque on dipole, when it is deflected by a small angle $\theta$ is $\tau=p E \sin \theta=p E \theta$ \{ for small angle, $\sin \theta=\theta\}$
But $\quad \tau=I \alpha$
where, $\quad I=m r^2+m r^2=2 m r^2$
So, angular acceleration of dipole is
$$
\alpha=\frac{p E \theta}{I}
$$
Time to align back with field is $\left(\right.$ using $\left.\theta=\frac{1}{2} \alpha t^2\right)$
$$
t=\sqrt{\frac{2 \theta}{\alpha}}=\sqrt{\frac{2 \theta I}{p E \theta}}=\sqrt{\frac{2 I}{P E}}=\sqrt{50} \approx 2 \cdot 5 \pi(\mathrm{s})
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A uniformly charged spherical shell of radius R carries a uniform charge density of σ per unit area. It is made of two hemispherical shells, held together by pressing them with force F. F is proportional to
questions.solution:
The electrostatic pressure at a point on the charged conducting surface of surface charge density σ is dFdA=σ22ε0

Due to the electrostatic repulsion between the charged hemisphere, there is a requirement of an external force F on each hemisphere which will balance the electrostatic force due to the other hemisphere.

For the given case, consider a thin ring at an angle θ away from the diametrical axis. Now lets take a small ring of angle dθ around this ring, as shown in the figure below :

Let dA be the area element corresponding to the small ring such that dA=2πRsinθ×Rdθ

Now due to the electrostatic force, there will be an electrostatic pressure on the whole sphere. For the small ring dF=σ22ε0×dA

Component of dF along vertical axis will be dFcosθ. By symmetry, all horizontal components will cancel each other.

Therefore, total force :

F=∫0π2dFcosθ⇒F=∫0π2σ22ε02πR2sinθcosθdθ

⇒F=∫0π2σ22ε0πR2sin2θdθ

⇒F=σ22ε0πR2-cos2θ20π2

⇒F=σ22ε0πR2

⇒F∝σ2ε0R2
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Two charged particles each of mass $9.8 \mathrm{~g}$ and charges $+20 \mu \mathrm{C}$ and $-20 \mu \mathrm{C}$ are attached to the two ends of a massless and rigid uniform non-conducting rod of length $50 \mathrm{~cm}$. This arrangement is held in a uniform electric field of $12.1 \mathrm{NC}^{-1}$, such that the rod makes a very small angle with the field direction. If the rod is set free, the minimum time needed for the rod to become parallel to the direction of the electric field is ................ seconds.
questions.solution: This arrangement of electric dipole in electric field will undergo SHM, when the rod is set free. Time period of this SHM is given by $$ T=2 \pi \sqrt{\frac{I}{\mathrm{PE}}} $$ where, $I=$ moment of inertia of dipole, $p=$ electric dipole moment and $E=$ electric field. $I=2 m r^2$ $$ \begin{aligned} & \text { mass, } m=9.8 \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{~kg}, r=\frac{50}{2} \times 10^{-2} \\ & I=2 \times 9.8 \times 10^{-3} \times\left(\frac{50}{2} \times 10^{-2}\right)^2 \\ & I=1225 \times 10^{-4} \mathrm{k}-g^2 m^{-2}=10^{-5} \mathrm{C}-\mathrm{m} \\ & p=q(2 l)=20 \times 10^{-6} \times 50 \times 10^{-2} \\ & E=121 \mathrm{~N} / \mathrm{C} \\ & T=2 \pi \sqrt{\frac{1225 \times 10^{-4}}{10^{-5} \times 121}}=2 \times 3.14 \times 3.181 \\ & T=19.98 \cong 20 \mathrm{~s} \end{aligned} $$ But time taken by the rod to become parallel will be $T / 4$, so required time is $5 \mathrm{~s}$.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Four positive point charges <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>+</mo><mi>q</mi></math> are kept at the four corners of a square of side <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>l</mi></math>. The net electric field at the midpoint of any one side of the square is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo></math>take <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mrow><mn>4</mn><mi>π</mi><msub><mo>∈</mo><mn>0</mn></msub></mrow></mfrac><mo>=</mo><mi>k</mi></math>)
questions.solution:
The electric field of EC & ED are equal and opposite so they cancel out each other,

now net electric field at the midpoint of one side is,

2EAcosθ because EA=EB, so,

Enet=2×k×ql2+l22cosθEnet=2×k×ql2+l222×ll2+l22Enet=16 kq55l2
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: If an uncharged sphere of metal is placed in the uniform electric field produced by two large oppositely charged plates, then the lines of force will look like
questions.solution: Due to induction charges will appear on the surface of sphere as shown in diagram. Electric field lines never enters any conducting surface and are perpendicular at the surface.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Two concentric conducting spheres of radii r1 and r2r1 < r2 carry electric charges of + Q and -Q respectively. The region between the sphere is filled with two insulating layers of dielectric constant ε1 and ε2 and width d1 and d2 respectively. Variation of potential and electric field with radial distance from O is given. Select the correct one. (assume Vat r2=0)
questions.solution:
Lets take value of Electric field in given regions asE1,E2,E3 and E4 respectively.

For r<  r1, net charge enclosed by the gaussian surface will be zero.
∴E1=0

For r1 < r < r1 + d, from the concept of di-electrics we know that electric field in the presence of dielectric will be reduced by a factor equal to dielectric constant.
∴ E2=Q4πε0r2ε1

Same way for r1 + d < r < r2,

E3=Q4πε0ε2r2

Fro r > r2, if we draw a spherical gaussian surface, net charge enclosed by it would be zero. Hence electric field outside r2 distance from the center will be zero.
∴E4=0
Potential is given by V=-∫∞rE→.dr→……i
Since electric field is zero outside r2 and upto infinity so potential will be zero between r2 and ∞.
i.e. V=-∫∞rE.dr=0

Electric field depends on 1r2  between r1 to r2, from equation i potential also varies between r1 to r2 and from r<r1 , electric field is zero. Hence pototential will be constant in that region.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Consider a solid cube made up of insulating material having a uniform volume charge density. Assuming the electrostatic potential to be zero at infinity, the ratio of the  potential at a corner of the cube to that at the centre will be
questions.solution:
ρ-Charge density of the cube

Vlcorner- Potential at the corner of a cube of side l

Vlcentre- Potential at the centre of a cube of side l

Vl/2centre- Potential at the center of a cube of side l2

Vl/2corner- Potential at the corner of a cube of side l2

By dimensional analysis

Vlcorner∝Ql=ρl2

Vlcorner=4 Vl/2corner

But by superposition Vlcentre=8Vl/2corner

Because of the centre of the larger cube lies at a corner of the eight smaller cubes of which it is made

Therefore, VlcornerVlcentre=4Vl/2corner8Vl/2corner=12
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
